raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California will soon make free breakfast and lunch available to all public school students.",
    "SB 1481 would ensure that all families can send their kids to child care centers offering free, nutritious meals.",
    "The Food with Care bill would remove the financial burden on child care providers who currently aren’t fully reimbursed for providing meals to children.",
    "SB 1481 enables preschool-age kids to have access to free meals by changing California’s rules for a federally funded nutrition program.",
    "The unreimbursed balance of meal costs for daycare providers amounts to more than $34 million annually.",
    "Child care providers who cannot afford the costs typically opt out of the reimbursement program and offer no meals, or only a snack, to children.",
    "The legislation aims to address historical wage inequities of the essential child care workforce.",
    "The Food with Care Bill would help serve high-quality food, focusing on organic foods, fresh fruit, vegetables, and more protein at breakfasts.",
    "The Food with Care bill will substantially increase monthly CACFP reimbursement for daycare centers.",
    "A higher reimbursement will allow for an increase in the frequency of serving whole grains, fresh fruit, and fresh vegetables daily."
  ]
}
```
